summary: Scorching Wharf Rat, soaring Estimated Prophet and tight Deal
blurb: The show shines with a fiery Wharf Rat solo, a soaring Estimated Prophet and a punchy Deal finale. Jerry’s refurbished Wolf guitar adds rich tone throughout, while Donna Jean’s vocals lift Must Have Been Roses and the Prophet. Overall performance is strong despite a few uneven drum moments.
review:
April 22, 1978 at Nashville Municipal Auditorium captured one of the spring‑'78 high points for the Dead. The first set rolled out classic crowd‑pleasers—Bertha, Good Lovin', Candyman and a bright Deal that closed the set with Bob’s trademark “We quit…see ya later!” banter. The second set took off with Lazy Lightning into a mesmerizing Estimated Prophet, where Jerry’s newly refurbished Wolf guitar sang over Donna Jean’s soaring harmonies, then built to an unforgettable Wharf Rat jam that many reviewers called the highest‑guitar peak ever heard. A swinging Sugar Magnolia wrapped it up, and the encore One More Saturday Night kept the energy high.

Across all available sources the band is praised for being “on fire.” Jerry’s tone was described as rich, frothy and full of jazz‑cat riffs, especially on Wharf Rat where his solo blazed. Donna Jean’s vocal contributions shine on Must Have Been Roses and the Prophet, adding a bright layer to the mix. While the drum work drew mixed notes—some felt it weak early in Eyes of the World, others celebrated long, cool percussion passages—the overall flow was lively and cohesive, making this a must‑listen show for any Deadhead.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All four recordings are marked positive, repeatedly praising Jerry’s tone and solos, the soaring Estimated Prophet, the scorching Wharf Rat, and overall band chemistry. No negative sentiment appears across analyses, so a strong positive adjustment is applied.

best_recording.reason: Crystal‑clear soundboard capture with excellent instrument separation, no dropouts, and faithful representation of the band’s dynamic performance.

key_highlights: Scorching Wharf Rat solo that many call Jerry’s highest‑guitar peak.
Soaring Estimated Prophet featuring Jerry’s refurbished Wolf tone and Donna Jean’s harmonies.
Punchy Deal finale with Bob’s classic “We quit…see ya later!” intro.
Extended, cool drum passages praised in the audience tape version.
song_highlights: It Must Have Been The Roses

band_performance.Jerry: Rich, frothy tone on his refurbished Wolf guitar; jazz‑cat riffs throughout, scorching Wharf Rat solo and an intense final solo that lifted the whole show.
band_performance.Bob: Vocal intro on Deal (“We quit. See ya later!”) and classic stage banter at the close; solid rhythm guitar work supporting the jams.
band_performance.Keith: Donna Jean’s vocal harmonies shine on Must Have Been Roses and Estimated Prophet, while Keith provides steady keyboard support throughout.
band_performance.Mickey: Long, cool drum passages highlighted as a strong point, especially in the extended second‑set jam sections.
band_performance.Billy: Percussion contributed to mixed reactions—some uneven moments early in Eyes of the World but overall solid drive behind the band's energy.

must_listen_sequences: It Must Have Been The Roses
